components.name: processConfig
components.description: Use this node to represent a configuration of the process. This node has no effect besides documentation. It should not have any inputs or outputs as it will not be rendered as a node in the executable model.
components.usage: Use this node to represent a configuration of the process. This node has no effect besides documentation. It should not have any inputs or outputs as it will not be rendered as a node in the executable model.

requiredInputs.name: processId
requiredInputs.description: Unique identifier for the process instance

requiredInputs.name: processName
requiredInputs.description: Name of the process appearing to the user. This should contain contextual information to allow instances of a process to be distinguished

requiredInputs.name: processDescription
requiredInputs.description: Name of the process appearing to the user. This should contain contextual information to allow instances of a process to be distinguished

requiredInputs.name: processDeadline
requiredInputs.description: Expected completion date and time for the process in date format (e.g. YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) or  duration (e.g. P{days}DT{hours}H{minutes}M)

requiredInputs.name: maximumActiveProcesses
requiredInputs.description: Maximum number of active processes allowed before throttling

components.name: unknownElement
components.description: Use this node to represent a step if there is no applicable component in the rest of the library.  This node has no effect besides documentation.  Its single output should connect to the next node in the model.  Do not use this for generic logic (checking values, parsing text etc.)
components.usage: Use this node to represent a step if there is no applicable component in the rest of the library.  This node has no effect besides documentation.  Its single output should connect to the next node in the model.  Do not use this for generic logic (checking values, parsing text etc.)
